Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: this is a detailed supply chain scenario with multiple layers

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The user wants 20 open ended questions that dig deeper into insights risks and improvements

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The questions should avoid being too technical or SQL focused keeping them strategic and exploratory

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should cover different phases of the supply chain design sourcing logistics distribution and broader implications

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me think about pain points like geopolitical risks sustainability and digital integration

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The questions need to prompt analysis rather than yes no answers

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Maybe start with RD decisions since that sets the tone for the entire chain

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then move to supplier dependencies geopolitical tensions could disrupt components

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Logistics and inventory strategies are crucial for cost vs service trade offs

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Dont forget after sales and circular economy angles those are often overlooked but vital for long term resilience

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Ill weave in emerging trends like AI and blockchain but frame them as opportunities or challenges

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Each question should open up a discussion not just list a problem

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For example instead of What are the risks ask How might geopolitical shifts alter the supplier network to force deeper thinking
